# Copilot Instructions

## Commands

```sh
go build ./...                          # build
go test ./...                           # run all tests
go test ./users/... -run TestGetUser    # run a single test
go generate ./openapi/...               # regenerate types from OpenAPI spec
```

Run linting and tests before pushing:

```sh
golangci-lint run ./...
go test ./...
```

The CI pipeline runs `go build`, `golangci-lint`, and `go test` on every PR. PRs must have a [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/en/v1.0.0/) title for automated versioning.

Follow TDD when making changes: write a failing test first, then implement the minimum code to make it pass.

## Architecture

The top-level `Client` struct (in `client.go`, package `appstore`) is the public API. It wraps a `networking.HTTPClient` and delegates each operation to a sub-package function:

```
Client.GetUser(...)  →  users.Get(httpClient, ctx, baseURL, id)
```

Each resource lives in its own package (e.g. `users/`) with one file per operation (`get.go`, `create.go`, `modify.go`, `delete.go`, `find.go`). Operations are plain functions — not methods — that accept `networking.HTTPClient` as their first argument, making them easy to test without the top-level `Client`.

**User/invitation duality**: `users.Get` first calls `GET /v1/users/{id}`. On a 404 it transparently falls back to `GET /v1/userInvitations/{id}` and returns the same `User` struct with `HasAcceptedInvite: false`.

**Generated types**: JSON request/response types are generated by `oapi-codegen` from `openapi/spec.json` into `openapi/types.gen.go`. The spec is a hand-curated subset of Apple's App Store Connect API. To add types for a new resource, add the relevant operations to `openapi/spec.json` and `openapi/cfg.yaml`, then run `go generate ./openapi/...`.

**Public type aliases**: Where a resource package needs to expose a type from `openapi`, use a type alias (`type UserRole = openapi.UserRole`) rather than a new type or re-exported constants. The only consumer is [terraform-provider-appstore](https://github.com/Oliver-Binns/terraform-provider-appstore).

## Key Conventions

**HTTP response body closing**: Do not use `defer resp.Body.Close()` — the linter (`errcheck`) will fail. Close the body explicitly at each return path, using `_ = resp.Body.Close()` when discarding the error (e.g. on early error returns) and `if err := resp.Body.Close(); err != nil { return nil, err }` on the success path.

**Pointer helpers** (`internal/ptr`):
- `ptr.Deref(p)` — safely dereferences a pointer, returning the zero value if nil
- `ptr.PtrOrNil(v)` — returns a pointer to `v`, or `nil` if `v` is the zero value
- `ptr.SlicePtrOrNil(s)` — returns a pointer to a slice, or `nil` if it is empty

**Testing**: Tests use `mocknetworking.MockHTTPClient`, which replays a queue of `MockHTTPResponse` values in order. Use the helpers `MockHTTPClientWith200Response(body)` and `MockHTTPClientWithSingleResponse(statusCode, body)` for single-response cases. Use `assert.JSONEq` (not `assert.Equal`) when comparing JSON request bodies, as generated structs serialise fields in alphabetical order.
